images.altText: A satellite image of a cyclone with a well-defined eye and a large swirl of clouds.

image.altText: Heavy rain and strongs winds lash homes in Exmouth.

children.content: Exmouth bears the brunt of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it approaches the West Australian coast as a category three system, with early reports of widespread damage across the town.

items.title: WA coast braces for 'pretty significant damage' as Cyclone Narelle nears landfall

updates.title: Exmouth's evacuation centre roof partly blown off
Emergency warning for Barrow Island downgraded

items.title: No let-up from Cyclone Narelle as dangerous storm heads south

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to cut a destructive swathe through Western Australia's Gascoyne even as it weakens, with authorities warning the extreme weather is not over yet.

image.altText: Tin roofs collapsed on carports with a boat and car in the foreground.

items.title: Track Cyclone Narelle's path and live wind speeds in WA

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and monitor live wind speeds as it crosses the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

items.title: At 104, George is still setting the tempo as he closes in on world record

children.content: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

gallery.altText: An elderly man holds his arm up in front of an orchestra
An elderly man blows out the candles on a birthday cake as friends look on

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man holds his arm up in front of an orchestra
A man in a blue shirt sits conducting an orchestra
An elderly man blows out the candles on a birthday cake as friends look on

avatar.alt: A young woman named Lauren Smith wears a light coloured blazer over a black shirt.

items.title: Daily Perth-Rome flights introduced by Qantas as Middle East routes collapse

children.content: Qantas will increase capacity on flights between Australia and Europe after Easter, with more flights between Perth and Rome, in a move experts say may see the "kangaroo route" evolve.

gallery.altText: the tail of a Qantas Boeing 767
A screenshot of FlightRadar24, showing no planes over the Middle East and Ukraine and lots of flights over Central Asia.

thumbnail.altText: the tail of a Qantas Boeing 767
A screenshot of FlightRadar24, showing no planes over the Middle East and Ukraine and lots of flights over Central Asia.

avatar.alt: A white woman with long blonde hair sitting in a TV studio and smiling

items.title: Is the end of the Iran war near?

children.content: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily

children.content: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a suit and glasses, standing in front an Australian flag with his hand on his stomach.

items.title: Katherine's worst flood in 28 years leaves survivors with disaster 'PTSD'

children.content: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

image.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.

items.title: Diesel soaring faster at the bowser than petrol, ACCC finds

children.content: Meanwhile the ASX is trading down on Friday, following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The indicator board at the Australian Stock Exchange displaying red

items.title: Inflation set to soar as fuel costs hit prices of everyday items from bread to ridesharing

children.content: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

gallery.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.
A van parked in front of a business.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.
A van parked in front of a business.

items.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

items.title: Fremantle among defendants in expanding AFL concussion class action

children.content: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

items.title: Weekend line-up: Scarpetta, Belle Burden and hot cross buns

children.content: Fire up your remotes, headphones or over and get set for a weekend of entertainment.

image.altText: A TV still of Nicole Kidman, 58, in glasses, a medical gown and mask in a room with medical equipment.

items.title: Hard time for renters as landlords squeeze tight market

children.content: Australian renters continue to have a hard time finding a home, despite vacancy rates slowly improving from post COVID pandemic lows, a report finds.

image.altText: A For Lease sign advertising a rental property in Carlton North.

items.title: Review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws

children.content: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

gallery.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

thumbnail.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and Donald Trump's social faux pas

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: Superannuation giant HESTA's new admin provider on brink of collapse

children.content: Experts are concerned one of Australia's largest super funds has invested in a new admin company whose most recent financial report indicates it is insolvent.

items.title: AOC backs Olympic transgender rules despite human rights concerns

children.content: The Australian Olympic Committee supports new guidelines for transgender participation at the Olympic Games, as human rights experts and LGBTQIA+ advocates raise alarm bells.

image.altText: A woman wearing an Australian Olympic team shirt gestures as she speaks into a microphone at a press conference.

items.title: 'Reliable evidence': Exercise halves early death risk of middle-aged women

children.content: Australian researchers found that middle-aged women who exercised regularly halved their risk of premature death. But most women aren't meeting physical activity guidelines.

image.altText: Woman jogging while listening music at beach against sky

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

Nazeem Hussain and Virginia Trioli sit at a cafe table smiling, papers and photos in front of them
Nazeem Hussain on stage with big lights behind him, holding a microphone and smiling
